pr.title: Split, name, & link files from gm_1A4C
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Split substantial game-mode code out of the monolithic src/melee/gm/gm_1A4C.c into newly named and linked units, especially gmmovieend, gmregtyfall, gmopening, gmstaffroll, gmhowto, gmomake15, and gmvsdata. The PR updated GALE01 section splits, configure.py object registration, symbols metadata, and related headers/prototypes so the moved code could link under separate object names. decomp.dev reported net progress despite expected apparent broken matches in the old gm_1A4C unit: matched code +3012 bytes, linked code +5964 bytes, 44 new matches, and several newly split nonmatching functions with high partial match percentages.
what_changed: Reduced src/melee/gm/gm_1A4C.c by nearly a thousand lines and left it as a smaller residual unit while moving large contiguous ranges into dedicated game-mode files.
Added new source files for gmregtyfall, gmstaffroll, gmopening, gmmovieend, gmhowto, gmomake15, and gmvsdata, with matching new headers for several of those units.
Updated config/GALE01/splits.txt to carve the old gm_1A4C address range into per-file .text, .data, .bss, .sdata, .sbss, .sdata2, and .rodata ranges.

Updated configure.py so gmvsmelee became Matching and the newly split files were registered with their current status: gmvsdata, gmmovieend, gmhowto, and gmomake15 as Matching; gmregtyfall, gmopening, gmstaffroll, and remaining gm_1A4C as NonMatching.
Removed old gm_1A4C.static.h content and reduced reliance on the previous gm_1A4C umbrella header by adding split-unit headers instead.
Corrected gm_SetScenePendingMinor from int pending_minor to u8 pending_minor in both implementation and header.
Added an extern declaration for gm_804D6724 in gm_1A45.h and adjusted local declaration evidence in gm_1A4C.c.
Updated symbols.txt to mark gm_803DB2EC as data:float.
Changed .clang-format IncludeCategories priority for quoted *.static.h includes from 3 to 1.

Touched neighboring gm files such as gm_1B03.c, gmsupersudden.c, gmfixedcamera.c, gmsinglebutton.c, gmlightning.c, gmgiant.c, gminvisible.c, gmslomo.c, gmtiny.c, gmstamina.c, gmhomerun.c, gmvsmelee.c, gmvsmelee.h, gmscdata.c, gmvsdata.h, lbmthp.h, and sobjlib.h to consume the new declarations and split layout.
smart_moves: Used the linker split configuration as the source of truth: the PR did not merely rename files, it introduced exact section ranges in splits.txt for every extracted unit.
Preserved progress accounting by registering each new object with an explicit Matching or NonMatching state in configure.py, making partial splits visible to decomp.dev.

Named extracted files after recognizable game-mode responsibilities rather than keeping address-only names, improving searchability and future ownership of movie ending, regular trophy fall, opening, staff roll, how-to, Omake 15, and VS data code.
Moved data alongside code into the split units, including data arrays and THP filename tables, instead of leaving all globals in the residual gm_1A4C file.
Used clang-format off/on around large ordered string tables in the diff excerpt, which helps preserve readable one-entry-per-line data layout and avoids formatting churn in data-sensitive arrays.
Made small type/header fixes during the split, such as narrowing gm_SetScenePendingMinor to u8 and annotating gm_803DB2EC as float data, reducing future mismatch and declaration ambiguity.
decomp_lessons: When splitting a monolithic decomp unit, expect decomp.dev to report broken matches in the old unit and new matches in the new units; judge the PR by net linked/matched code and by whether symbols moved to the intended objects.
Do not split only .text: data, bss, small-data, small-bss, sdata2, and rodata ranges must move with the code to keep object layout and references coherent.
After a split, immediately add or adjust headers for cross-object declarations; otherwise neighboring game-mode files will keep depending on stale umbrella/static headers.
Address-derived filenames like gm_1A4C are useful staging areas, but named units such as gmopening.c and gmstaffroll.c make future matching and review much easier.
Type tightening on public prototypes, such as int to u8 for scene-minor state, can be part of split cleanup when the call sites and stored field width support it.
Symbol metadata matters for matching and search: marking data objects as float arrays documents intent and helps later data reconstruction.
naming_conventions: New game-mode files use lowercase gm-prefixed names without underscores for identified modes: gmmovieend.c, gmregtyfall.c, gmopening.c, gmstaffroll.c, gmhowto.c, gmomake15.c, gmvsdata.c.
Lifecycle suffixes such as _OnEnter and _OnFrame remain on functions after moving them into named files, e.g. gm_801A64A8_OnFrame, gm_801AA110_OnEnter, gm_801ACD8C_OnFrame.
Unidentified helper functions retain address-based names such as fn_801A6664 and gm_801A6EE4 rather than receiving speculative names.
Global symbols continue to use address-based gm_80xxxxxx names where semantic names are not proven, e.g. gm_804D6724 and gm_803DB2EC.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Split the original gm_1A4C range into contiguous per-object address ranges in config/GALE01/splits.txt, including exact text and data boundaries for the new units.
Updated configure.py object status in lockstep with splits so the build knew which newly split objects were Matching versus NonMatching.

Kept nonmatching extracted functions in their new logical units, allowing decomp.dev to track improvements like gmregtyfall::gm_801A7070_OnEnter at 81.40%, gmstaffroll::gm_801AC6D8_OnEnter at 80.60%, gm_1A4C::gm_801A9B30_OnEnter at 97.48%, and gmstaffroll::fn_801AA854 at 94.87%.
Used data typing metadata in symbols.txt, changing gm_803DB2EC to data:float, which is useful when reconstructing float tables and matching data sections.
Moved or exposed externs such as gm_804D6724 as part of the split so cross-object references could link cleanly.
Adjusted include ordering rules for *.static.h in .clang-format, suggesting static headers should be grouped earlier with local includes for decomp-style include organization.

review_feedback.feedback: GALE01 report showed matched code increased to 37.70% (+0.08%, +3012 bytes) and linked code increased to 25.48% (+0.15%, +5964 bytes).

Reported 44 new matches, mainly in the newly split units, including gmopening::gm_801AA28C_OnFrame, gmregtyfall::fn_801A6664, gmregtyfall::gm_801A6EE4, gmopening::gm_801AA110_OnEnter, and gmmovieend/gmhowto/gmomake15 functions.
Reported 36 broken matches in old gm_1A4C, consistent with code being moved out of that object rather than necessarily regressed.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

searchable_terms: Split name link files from gm_1A4C
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching gmregtyfall::gm_801A7070_OnEnter after this split.
Find later PRs that finish matching gmstaffroll::gm_801AC6D8_OnEnter or fn_801AA854.
Search for further cleanup of gm_1A4C residual functions after gmmovieend/gmregtyfall/gmopening/gmstaffroll extraction.
Search for uses of gm_SetScenePendingMinor after the u8 signature change.
Find PRs that remove or reorganize gm_1A4C.static.h after changing .clang-format static header priority.
